calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Blocked Account Receipts
Off Calendar Jan-22-2024 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-08-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Jodi Phillips appeared via zoom for Eric Phan (petitioner).
Lee Maranto appeared via zoom for (minors).

Discussion was held regarding custodial assets of the minors, that the primary concern is the maintenance that's involved in guardianship, and that the best option is to place the funds in a blocked account until the minors turn 18 years old. The Court discharges Lee Maranto as guardian ad litem. The first and final l report of administrator etc., is taken under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Ross Moody: Clerk: A. Datt: Deputy: Cabrera: Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-21-2022 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Mar-21-2022 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-01-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Eric Phan, via ZOOM, in propria persona, Petitioner; Lee Maranto, via ZOOM, Guardian Ad Litem.

The court queries Mr. Phan regarding whether he has ever administrated an estate. Mr. Phan reports that he has not but has friends who are attorneys who have helped him. The court then confirms that Mr. Phan does not intend to sell the Elk Grove property. The court questions Mr. Maranto regarding his suggestion as to Mr. Phan's authority if his petition is granted. Mr. Maranto reports that he would want Mr. Phan to have authority to pay the mortgage. Mr. Maranto reports that there is a plan to structure a buy-out provision to purchase the minors' stake of the real property and prefers that the court give Mr. Phan full authority to avoid delays in administering the estate. After brief discussion, the court advises that it intends to appoint Mr. Phan as Administrator with full authority; bond will be fixed at $390,000; Michal Kasolas is appointed probate referee. A Status on the Filing of an Inventory and Appraisal is set for Mar-21-2022 at 9:00 am in Department 204 and Status on Close of Estate is set for Nov-21-2022 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

The Petition for Letters of Administration is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-27-2021 Continued To Nov-01-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Off Calendar Aug-17-2021 Continued To Sep-14-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jul-06-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Eric Phan, via ZOOM, in propria persona, Petitioner; Lee Maranto, via ZOOM, Guardian Ad Litem.

The court begins by advising Mr. Phan that it is inclined to adopt its tentative ruing to deny this petition without prejudice. Mr. Phan advises that he is agreement with Mr. Maranto's report.

After additional discussion, the Spousal Property Petition is denied without prejudice in open court. A Status on Petition for Probate is set for August 17, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: C. Lee. Not reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Mar-30-2021 Continued To May-25-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Eric Phan appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

The Court asked for more information about Ms. Ly's ties to San Francisco, stated that it has to establish that Ms. Ly was domicile in San Francisco at the time of death and asked if there is a probate for Ms. Ly. Mr. Phan stated that they have been living here for ten years, that they were married in 2008, that their two kids were born in San Francisco, that they recently moved to Elk Grove and that they both grew up in San Francisco. Mr. Phan also stated that he does not think an estate has been open for Ms. Ly, that they own a couple more homes, that he created a living trust, that he recently sold one property that was held as community property with right of survivorship and that they owned everything else together. The Court informed Mr. Phan that it will require a Guardian Ad Litem ("GAL") for the Children, stated that his interest is in conflict with the Children's interest and that the Children would inherit more if the Elk Grove property was Ms. Ly's separate property. The Court also informed Mr. Phan that the GAL needs to be an independent person who is a Lawyer, or a neutral person who is represented by a Lawyer and that they will need to file a petition for appointment of the GAL. Mr. Phan stated that he is concerned about the mortgage payments, that the mortgage was set up for auto pay from Ms. Ly's own checking account and that he is concerned that the funds will might run out. The Court suggested that Mr. Phan may want to have a lawyer as well. The Petition for Spousal Property is continued to May 25, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
